# quent

> Transparent sync/async bridge for Python. Write pipeline code once -- quent detects awaitables at runtime and handles them automatically. Pure Python, zero dependencies, PEP 561 typed.

quent provides a fluent `Chain` class for building sequential pipelines. The same chain definition works for both sync and async callables with no code duplication. Execution starts synchronously; if any step returns an awaitable, quent seamlessly transitions to an async path. `.run()` returns either a plain value or a coroutine -- the caller decides whether to `await`.

- Package: quent
- Python: >=3.10
- License: MIT
- Dependencies: None (pure Python on 3.11+; typing_extensions on 3.10)
- Typing: PEP 561 (py.typed + inline annotations)
- Install: `pip install quent`
- GitHub: https://github.com/drukmano/quent
- Docs: https://quent.readthedocs.io
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/quent/

## Documentation

- [Full Documentation](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/drukmano/quent)
- [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/quent/)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/drukmano/quent/releases)

## Quick Start

```python
from quent import Chain

# Basic pipeline
result = Chain([1, 2, 3]).foreach(lambda x: x * 2).then(sum).run()
# 12

# Same chain works with async callables -- no changes needed
result = await Chain(url).then(fetch).then(process).run()

# Side effects with .do() -- result discarded, value passes through
result = Chain(data).do(log).then(transform).do(save).run()

# Error handling -- handler receives ChainExcInfo(exc, root_value)
result = Chain(url).then(fetch).except_(lambda ei: default_value).run()

# Reuse via clone
base = Chain().then(validate).then(transform)
branch_a = base.clone().then(save_to_db)
branch_b = base.clone().then(save_to_file)
```

## The Bridge Contract

> Given a pipeline of N steps, replacing any step's callable with a functionally equivalent callable of the opposite sync/async kind produces the same observable result.

Execution starts synchronously. On first awaitable result, transitions to async and stays async. A fully sync pipeline has zero async overhead. `.run()` returns a plain value or a coroutine.

## Calling Conventions

Two rules, applied in strict priority order. First match wins. These same 2 rules apply universally to ALL contexts: standard steps, except_() handlers, finally_() handlers, and if_() predicates.

| Priority | Rule | Trigger | Invocation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Explicit args | Args/kwargs provided | `fn(*args, **kwargs)` -- current value NOT passed |
| 2 | Default | None of the above | `fn(cv)`, `fn()` if no value, or `v` as-is if non-callable |

```python
Chain(5).then(str).run()                    # str(5) -- Rule 2
Chain(5).then(print, 'hello').run()         # print('hello') -- Rule 1
Chain(5).then(42).run()                     # 42 -- non-callable, Rule 2
```

## Key Concepts

- **Null sentinel** distinguishes "no value" from `None`. `Chain()` = no root value. `Chain(None)` = root value `None`. Never exposed to user code.
- **Current value** flows through the pipeline -- each step receives it and (unless side-effect) its result becomes the new current value.
- **Root value** seeds the pipeline: `Chain(v)` at build time, `chain.run(v)` at run time (overrides build-time root).

## API Overview

### Constructor and Execution

- [Chain(v=<no value>, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#constructor) -- Constructor with optional root value.
- [.run(v=<no value>, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#run) -- Execute chain. Returns value or coroutine.
- [chain(...)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#__call__) -- Alias for `.run()`.

### Pipeline Building

All methods return `self` for fluent chaining.

- [.then(v, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#then) -- Append step; result replaces current value.
- [.do(fn, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#do) -- Side-effect step; result discarded. `fn` must be callable.
- [.foreach(fn, *, concurrency=None, executor=None)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#foreach) -- Apply fn to each element, collect results.
- [.foreach_do(fn, *, concurrency=None, executor=None)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#foreach_do) -- Side-effect per element, keep originals.
- [.gather(*fns, concurrency=-1, executor=None)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#gather) -- Run multiple fns concurrently on current value. Always concurrent.
- [.with_(fn, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#with_) -- Enter current value as context manager, call fn. Result replaces current value.
- [.with_do(fn, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#with_do) -- Context manager side-effect variant. Result discarded.
- [.if_(predicate=None, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#if_) -- Begin conditional; must be followed by `.then()` or `.do()`.
- [.else_(v, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#else_) -- Else branch for preceding if_().
- [.else_do(fn, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#else_do) -- Side-effect else branch.
- [.name(label)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#name) -- Assign a label for traceback identification.

### Context API

- [.set(key)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#set-instance----pipeline-step) / [.set(key, value)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#set-instance----pipeline-step) -- Pipeline step: store current value or explicit value in execution context. Current value unchanged.
- [.get(key)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#get-instance----pipeline-step) / [.get(key, default)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#get-instance----pipeline-step) -- Pipeline step: retrieve value from context. Replaces current value.
- [Chain.set(key, value)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#set-class----immediate) -- Immediate: store value in context at call site.
- [Chain.get(key)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#get-class----immediate) / [Chain.get(key, default)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#get-class----immediate) -- Immediate: retrieve from context at call site.

### Error Handling

- [.except_(fn, *args, exceptions=None, reraise=False, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#except_) -- Exception handler (one per chain). Handler receives `ChainExcInfo(exc, root_value)` as current value.
- [.finally_(fn, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#finally_) -- Cleanup handler (one per chain, always runs). Receives root value. Return always discarded.

### Iteration

- [.iterate(fn=None)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#iterate) -- Dual sync/async iterator over chain output.
- [.iterate_do(fn=None)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#iterate_do) -- Iterator with side-effect fn; original elements yielded.
- [.flat_iterate(fn=None, *, flush=None)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#flat_iterate) -- Flatmap iterator; flattens one level or maps fn to sub-iterables. Optional `flush` emits remaining items after source ends.
- [.flat_iterate_do(fn=None, *, flush=None)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#flat_iterate_do) -- Like flat_iterate but fn results discarded; original elements yielded.

### Reuse

- [.clone()](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#clone) -- Independent copy of chain. Structure deep-copied, callables shared.
- [.decorator()](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#decorator) -- Wrap chain as function decorator.
- [Chain.from_steps(*steps)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#from_steps) -- Construct chain from a sequence of `.then()` steps. Single list/tuple arg auto-unpacked.

### Control Flow

- [Chain.return_(v=<no value>, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#return_) -- Early return from chain. Propagates through nested chains.
- [Chain.break_(v=<no value>, *args, **kwargs)](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#break_) -- Break from iteration; value appended to partial results.

### Instrumentation

- [Chain.on_step](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#on_step) -- Class-level step instrumentation callback. Supports two signatures:
  - 5 args: `(chain, step_name, input_value, result, elapsed_ns)` -- backward-compatible, not called on failure.
  - 6 args: `(chain, step_name, input_value, result, elapsed_ns, exception)` -- preferred, called on both success and failure.
  - Arity auto-detected via `inspect.signature` (cached). Zero overhead when `None`.

### Deferred with_ in Iteration

When `.with_(fn)` or `.with_do(fn)` is the last pipeline step before an iteration terminal (`iterate`, `iterate_do`, `flat_iterate`, `flat_iterate_do`), context manager entry is deferred to iteration time. The CM stays open for the duration of iteration and exits in the generator's `finally:` block. When both deferred `with_` and deferred `finally_()` are active, the CM exits first, then `finally_()` runs.

### Exports

- [Chain](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#chain) -- Main pipeline class.
- [ChainExcInfo](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#chainexcinfo) -- NamedTuple (exc, root_value) passed to except handlers.
- [ChainIterator](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#chainiterator) -- Type alias for `.iterate()` / `.iterate_do()` / `.flat_iterate()` / `.flat_iterate_do()` return values.
- [QuentException](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#quentexception) -- Exception type for quent-specific errors.
- [Null](https://quent.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/#null-sentinel) -- Internal sentinel for "no value provided".
- `__version__` -- Version string.

## Enhanced Tracebacks

When an exception occurs inside a chain, quent injects a `<quent>` frame into the traceback with chain visualization and `<----` marker on the failing step. Internal frames are cleaned. Named chains render as `Chain[label](root)`. Exception notes on Python 3.11+. Controlled by `QUENT_NO_TRACEBACK=1` and `QUENT_TRACEBACK_VALUES=0`.

## Concurrency

- `foreach`/`foreach_do`: `concurrency=None` (sequential, default), `-1` (unbounded), or positive integer.
- `gather`: always concurrent, `concurrency=-1` (unbounded, default) or positive integer.
- Sync: ThreadPoolExecutor. Async: Semaphore + TaskGroup (3.11+) / asyncio.gather (3.10).
- `executor` param on all three: optional user-provided Executor for sync path.
- Mixed sync/async within one concurrent operation raises TypeError.
- Works with asyncio, trio, and curio.

## Thread Safety

Building is not thread-safe. A fully constructed chain is safe to execute concurrently from multiple threads, including free-threaded Python (PEP 703).

## Optional

- [Source Code](https://github.com/drukmano/quent/tree/master/quent)
- [Issue Tracker](https://github.com/drukmano/quent/issues)
- [Full LLM Context (llms-full.txt)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/drukmano/quent/master/llms-full.txt): Complete API reference with all method signatures, calling conventions, and behavioral details.
